basic_details.job_title: MSC Bank Recruitment 2026 – 139 Posts | Trainee Junior Officer, Associate, Typist & Driver
basic_details.short_information: Maharashtra State Co-operative Bank Ltd. (MSC Bank) has released Advertisement No. 03/MSC Bank/2026-27 inviting online applications for 139 posts across four categories: Trainee Junior Officers (49), Trainee Associates (81), Trainee Typists (04), and Trainee Drivers (05). The online application window opens on 11 June 2026 and the last date to apply and pay fees is 01 July 2026. Only residents of Maharashtra State are eligible to apply.

application_fee.details: Application fees are uniform for all candidate categories (no SC/ST/OBC fee waiver is mentioned in the official notification). Fees must be paid online only — no other mode is accepted — between 11 June 2026 and 01 July 2026. Fees are non-refundable once paid. | Payment can be made via Debit Cards (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard/Maestro), Credit Cards, Internet Banking, IMPS, Cash Cards, or Mobile Wallets. Bank transaction charges, if any, are to be borne by the candidate.

qualification.details: Eligibility requirements differ by post for both educational qualifications and age limits. All candidates must have passed the Matriculation Examination with Marathi as one of the subjects and must be domiciled in Maharashtra State (Domicile Certificate is mandatory). Age is calculated as on 01 June 2026. | Education: Graduate in any discipline with at least 50% marks. Candidates with Bachelors/Masters in Law or JAIIB/CAIIB/MSCIT certifications, or work experience in Treasury/International Banking will be preferred. Experience: Minimum 2 years' experience in Banking field, preferably in Urban/DCC Bank as an Officer (Officer Cadre — first level where Clerk level exists, or second level where it does not). Age Limit: Minimum 23 years – Maximum 35 years (born not earlier than 01 June 1991). | Education: Graduate in any discipline with at least 50% marks. Preference for Government Commercial Certificate in Marathi and English typing; proficiency in Computer Applications required. Experience: Not mandatory; preference for candidates with Co-operative Intern experience in StCB/DCC Banks. Age Limit: Minimum 21 years – Maximum 32 years (born not earlier than 01 June 1994). | Education: Graduate in any discipline with Marathi in Matriculation. Must possess Government Commercial Certificate Exam — 30 w.p.m. in Marathi typing and 40 w.p.m. in English typing. Proficiency in Word Processing and Spread Sheet required. Experience: Not mandatory; preference for DTP Operator experience. Preference for Marathi/English stenography course holders. Age Limit: Minimum 21 years – Maximum 28 years (born not earlier than 01 June 1998). | Education: Matriculation (S.S.C.) Examination with Marathi as one of the subjects and a valid LMV licence. Experience: Not mandatory. Age Limit: Minimum 18 years – Maximum 30 years (born not earlier than 01 June 1996). Special relaxation: Children of MSC Bank employees get 5 years relaxation on maximum age for Trainee Driver post.

salary_details.details: All selected candidates will be engaged as Trainees for 12 months, during which a monthly stipend is paid. After successful training, candidates are placed in the Bank's regular grade with revised emoluments. Post-confirmation, a probation of 6 months follows before permanent absorption.

selection_process: Marathi Language and Comprehension 40 80 Marathi only 25 Minutes
The selection methodology varies by post but all posts involve an online written examination conducted by IBPS

followed by an additional stage specific to the post. Candidates must score at least 50% (100 marks out of 200) in the written test for officer/associate posts and 50% (60 marks out of 120) for the Driver post to be shortlisted for the next round.
Online Written Examination by IBPS (160 Questions
120 Minutes) Personal Interview by MSC Bank Final merit based on combined scores of Exam + Interview
Exam is in English only. Negative marking applies: 1/4 penalty for 4-option questions; 1/5 penalty for 5-option questions.
120 Minutes) Personal Interview by MSC Bank
Phase I – Online Exam by IBPS
Phase II – Skill Test (Typing Test): Candidates qualifying Phase I with 100 marks must appear for a typing skill test. Final merit is based on the Skill Test result.
Online Written Test by IBPS (120 Questions
90 Minutes) — Bilingual (English & Marathi) except General English section Personal Interview / Driving Test by MSC Bank
how_to_apply: The entire application process is online through the MSC Bank career portal. Physical applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. Candidates should keep their scanned documents ready before beginning registration.
Photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm, passport style, colour, 200×230 pixels, 20–50 KB)
Signature (black ink on white paper, 140×60 pixels, 10–20 KB)
Left thumb impression (white paper, black/blue ink, 240×240 pixels, 20–50 KB)
Handwritten declaration in English on white paper with black ink (800×400 pixels, 50–100 KB)

Declaration text: "I, _______ (Name), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required."
Click "Click here for New Registration" and enter Name, Contact details, and Email ID. Note the Provisional Registration Number and Password sent via SMS and email.
Log in and fill the application form. Use "SAVE AND NEXT" to save progress at each step.
Upload Photograph, Signature, Left Thumb Impression, and Handwritten Declaration as per specifications.
Fill remaining application details and click "Validate Your Details" followed by "Save & Next".
Preview the complete application using the "Preview Tab" and verify all entries.
Proceed to the Payment Tab and pay the application fee online.
Click "Submit" and save/print the e-Receipt and application form for future reference.
Only one application per candidate is allowed. In case of multiple applications, only the latest will be retained and earlier fees will be forfeited.

content_sections.heading: MSC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates planning to apply for MSC Bank Recruitment 2026 should note the following critical dates. The application process is entirely online and no physical form will be accepted. All dates are final as per the official notification dated 11 June 2026. Downlaod MSC Bank Trainee Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

rows.event: Last Date to Pay Application Fee (Online)

content_sections.heading: MSC Bank Vacancy Details – Post-wise Breakdown
content_sections.text: MSC Bank has announced a total of 139 vacancies under four trainee categories for its 2026-27 recruitment drive. The administration reserves the right to increase or decrease the number of vacancies. The post-wise vacancy distribution is as follows: Note: 5% reservation in Trainee Associate, Trainee Typist, and Trainee Driver posts is available for wrestlers who have represented Maharashtra at International/National level competitions and won Gold medals or been awarded Maharashtra Kesri/Hind Kesri.

content_sections.heading: MSC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Eligibility requirements differ by post for both educational qualifications and age limits. All candidates must have passed the Matriculation Examination with Marathi as one of the subjects and must be domiciled in Maharashtra State (Domicile Certificate is mandatory). Age is calculated as on 01 June 2026. Education: Graduate in any discipline with at least 50% marks. Candidates with Bachelors/Masters in Law or JAIIB/CAIIB/MSCIT certifications, or work experience in Treasury/International Banking will be preferred. Experience: Minimum 2 years' experience in Banking field, preferably in Urban/DCC Bank as an Officer (Officer Cadre — first level where Clerk level exists, or second level where it does not). Age Limit: Minimum 23 years – Maximum 35 years (born not earlier than 01 June 1991). Education: Graduate in any discipline with at least 50% marks. Preference for Government Commercial Certificate in Marathi and English typing; proficiency in Computer Applications required. Experience: Not mandatory; preference for candidates with Co-operative Intern experience in StCB/DCC Banks. Age Limit: Minimum 21 years – Maximum 32 years (born not earlier than 01 June 1994). Education: Graduate in any discipline with Marathi in Matriculation. Must possess Government Commercial Certificate Exam — 30 w.p.m. in Marathi typing and 40 w.p.m. in English typing. Proficiency in Word Processing and Spread Sheet required. Experience: Not mandatory; preference for DTP Operator experience. Preference for Marathi/English stenography course holders. Age Limit: Minimum 21 years – Maximum 28 years (born not earlier than 01 June 1998). Education: Matriculation (S.S.C.) Examination with Marathi as one of the subjects and a valid LMV licence. Experience: Not mandatory. Age Limit: Minimum 18 years – Maximum 30 years (born not earlier than 01 June 1996). Special relaxation: Children of MSC Bank employees get 5 years relaxation on maximum age for Trainee Driver post.

content_sections.heading: MSC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee
content_sections.text: Application fees are uniform for all candidate categories (no SC/ST/OBC fee waiver is mentioned in the official notification). Fees must be paid online only — no other mode is accepted — between 11 June 2026 and 01 July 2026. Fees are non-refundable once paid. Payment can be made via Debit Cards (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard/Maestro), Credit Cards, Internet Banking, IMPS, Cash Cards, or Mobile Wallets. Bank transaction charges, if any, are to be borne by the candidate.

content_sections.heading: MSC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Salary / Stipend & Pay Scale
content_sections.text: All selected candidates will be engaged as Trainees for 12 months, during which a monthly stipend is paid. After successful training, candidates are placed in the Bank's regular grade with revised emoluments. Post-confirmation, a probation of 6 months follows before permanent absorption.

rows.emoluments_after_training_approx: Rs. 34,400/- per month + Rs. 650/- typist allowance

content_sections.heading: MSC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process
content_sections.text: The selection methodology varies by post but all posts involve an online written examination conducted by IBPS, followed by an additional stage specific to the post. Candidates must score at least 50% (100 marks out of 200) in the written test for officer/associate posts and 50% (60 marks out of 120) for the Driver post to be shortlisted for the next round. Online Written Examination by IBPS (160 Questions, 200 Marks, 120 Minutes) Personal Interview by MSC Bank Final merit based on combined scores of Exam + Interview Exam is in English only. Negative marking applies: 1/4 penalty for 4-option questions; 1/5 penalty for 5-option questions. Online Written Examination by IBPS (160 Questions, 200 Marks, 120 Minutes) Personal Interview by MSC Bank Phase I – Online Exam by IBPS Phase II – Skill Test (Typing Test) : Candidates qualifying Phase I with 100 marks must appear for a typing skill test. Final merit is based on the Skill Test result. Online Written Test by IBPS (120 Questions, 120 Marks, 90 Minutes) — Bilingual (English & Marathi) except General English section Personal Interview / Driving Test by MSC Bank

tables.caption: Phase I – Online Exam by IBPS

content_sections.heading: Examination Centres
content_sections.text: The online written test will be held at centres across Maharashtra. Candidates cannot request a change of centre once allotted. Personal Interview and Skill Test will be held at Mumbai. Exam Centres: Mumbai / Navi Mumbai / Thane / MMR Region, Pune, Nagpur, Nashik, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Nanded, and Kolhapur .

content_sections.heading: Service Bond / Consent Deposit
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering about post-appointment obligations should note that selected candidates must execute an undertaking on Rs. 500/- stamp paper committing to serve MSC Bank for a minimum of 4 years from the date of joining. In case of default, voluntary resignation, or termination before the expiry of 4 years, the candidate is required to pay Rs. 10,00,000/- (Ten Lakh) as liquidated damages and execute a surety bond for Rs. 1,00,000/- from two income tax payees as sureties.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for MSC Bank Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: The entire application process is online through the MSC Bank career portal. Physical applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. Candidates should keep their scanned documents ready before beginning registration. Documents to scan before applying: Photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm, passport style, colour, 200×230 pixels, 20–50 KB) Signature (black ink on white paper, 140×60 pixels, 10–20 KB) Left thumb impression (white paper, black/blue ink, 240×240 pixels, 20–50 KB) Handwritten declaration in English on white paper with black ink (800×400 pixels, 50–100 KB) Declaration text: "I, _______ (Name), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required." Step-by-step application process: Visit https://msc.bank.in/career and click "APPLY ONLINE" . Click "Click here for New Registration" and enter Name, Contact details, and Email ID. Note the Provisional Registration Number and Password sent via SMS and email. Log in and fill the application form. Use "SAVE AND NEXT" to save progress at each step. Upload Photograph, Signature, Left Thumb Impression, and Handwritten Declaration as per specifications. Fill remaining application details and click "Validate Your Details" followed by "Save & Next" . Preview the complete application using the "Preview Tab" and verify all entries. Click "COMPLETE REGISTRATION" after final verification. Proceed to the Payment Tab and pay the application fee online. Click "Submit" and save/print the e-Receipt and application form for future reference. Only one application per candidate is allowed. In case of multiple applications, only the latest will be retained and earlier fees will be forfeited.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid
content_sections.text: Many applicants lose their candidature due to avoidable errors during or after the application process. Review the following checklist before submitting your form. Applying for multiple posts: MSC Bank allows one application per candidate. Submit only one application for the post you are most qualified for. Wrong photograph format: Passport-style colour photo against a light background is required. Selfies, group photos, or blurred images will lead to rejection. Signature in CAPITAL LETTERS: Signatures in block/capital letters are strictly rejected. Sign in your normal cursive style with black ink. Missing Domicile Certificate: Only Maharashtra residents can apply. Ensure your Domicile Certificate is valid and ready for document verification. Not checking Marathi in SSC: All posts require Marathi as a subject in Matriculation. Check your mark sheet before applying. Pressing Back/Refresh during payment: After submitting payment information, do not press Back or Refresh. Wait for the server confirmation to avoid double charges. Incomplete scanning of thumb impression: The left thumb impression must be properly scanned and not smudged. A blurred or partial impression can invalidate your application. Not saving the e-Receipt: If an e-Receipt is not generated, the transaction may have failed. Log in again and repeat the payment process.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for MSC Bank Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application and pay the application fee is 01 July 2026. Applications received after this date will not be accepted.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in MSC Bank Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: MSC Bank has notified 139 vacancies in total — 49 for Trainee Junior Officers, 81 for Trainee Associates, 04 for Trainee Typists, and 05 for Trainee Drivers.

faq.question: Q3. Who is eligible to apply for MSC Bank Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Only residents of Maharashtra State are eligible. All posts require passing the Matriculation Examination with Marathi as a subject. For Trainee Junior Officer and Associate posts, graduation with minimum 50% marks is required. Trainee Drivers only need SSC pass with a valid LMV licence.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for MSC Bank 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee for Trainee Junior Officer is Rs. 1,770/- (including GST). For Trainee Associate, Trainee Typist, and Trainee Driver, the fee is Rs. 1,180/- each (including GST). Payment is online only; fees are non-refundable.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for the Trainee Junior Officer post?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 23 and 35 years of age as on 01 June 2026, i.e., born on or after 01 June 1991.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for MSC Bank Trainee Junior Officer?
faq.answer: Selection involves an online written examination conducted by IBPS (200 marks, 120 minutes) followed by a Personal Interview by MSC Bank. Final merit is based on combined performance in both stages.

faq.question: Q7. Is there negative marking in MSC Bank exam 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, all online tests carry negative marking. For questions with four options, the penalty is 1/3 mark per wrong answer. For questions with five options, the penalty is 1/4 mark per wrong answer.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary for MSC Bank Trainee Junior Officer after training?
faq.answer: After successful completion of the 12-month training period, Trainee Junior Officers are placed in the Bank's regular grade with total emoluments of approximately Rs. 52,100/- per month. During training, a stipend of Rs. 30,000/- per month is paid.

faq.question: Q9. Where will the MSC Bank exam be held?
faq.answer: The online written test will be held at centres in Mumbai/Navi Mumbai/Thane/MMR Region, Pune, Nagpur, Nashik, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Nanded, and Kolhapur. Personal Interviews and Skill Tests will be held at Mumbai.

faq.question: Q10. What is the official website for MSC Bank Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The official website is https://msc.bank.in. The career/apply page is at https://msc.bank.in/career. For queries, candidates may email [email protected].

faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application and pay the application fee is 01 July 2026. Applications received after this date will not be accepted.
